description: Page type definitions for EXPOZY AI page generator. References components by ID from the component catalog. Contains page layouts, AI fill instructions, generation workflow, and runtime state.

generation_workflow.description: The AI generation pipeline follows these steps in order.

steps.name: analyze_prompt
steps.description: AI reads the user prompt and extracts: business type, company name, services offered, location, contact details, language, tone. Outputs a structured brief.

steps.name: select_pages
steps.description: AI analyzes the user prompt and business context to decide which pages to generate. The AI has full control over page selection — no pages are forced. Each page type has selection_guidance that helps the AI decide.

constraints: header and footer are always generated (structural, not content pages)
At minimum the AI should select homepage + at least the legal pages relevant to the business
rules.guidance: Each page_type has a selection_guidance field. The AI reads all of them and picks the set that best fits the business.

steps.name: generate_pages
steps.description: AI generates HTML for each selected page using the component catalog. Each page follows its page_type definition. Pages are generated in the order defined by generation_order.

steps.name: generate_header
steps.description: AI generates the header AFTER pages are decided. The navigation links reflect which pages were selected. The header uses the site_header component from the catalog with nav items matching the selected pages.

steps.name: generate_footer
steps.description: AI generates the footer AFTER pages are decided. Footer nav columns and legal links reflect the selected pages. Uses the site_footer component from the catalog.

business_context.description: Structured data extracted from the user prompt in step 1. The orchestrator injects this into each page type's content_placeholders.

homepage.name: Homepage
homepage.description: Main landing page. First impression — hero section, value proposition, services overview, social proof, newsletter, and CTA with optional meeting booking.

ai_fills.badge.text: Short trust signal from business context (e.g. '20+ years of experience', '1000+ satisfied clients')
ai_fills.title: Main value proposition headline based on business_type and services
ai_fills.description: Supporting paragraph explaining what the business does
ai_fills.image_alt: Descriptive alt text for the business

ai_fills.heading: Section heading (e.g. 'Why choose us')
ai_fills.items: 3 feature cards with SVG icons, titles, descriptions — derived from business strengths

ai_fills.heading: Section heading (e.g. 'Our services')
ai_fills.items: 2-4 service cards with images, titles, descriptions, links — from business_context.services
3-4 stat items (e.g. '500+ Projects', '98% Satisfaction'). Derive from business context or use plausible defaults.

sections.note: Rendered at page level outside section wrappers. Only if cta_section uses modal action.

meta.title: {company_name}
meta.description: AI generates meta description from business context

alpine_page_state.data.meeting_calendars: Calendar objects with days and time_slots

homepage.selection_guidance: The main landing page. Should almost always be included — it's the entry point for every website. Skip only if the user explicitly asks for a single-page app or non-standard structure.

about.name: About Page
about.description: Company story, values, team credibility, gallery of work, and testimonials.

ai_fills.title: About page headline (e.g. company name or mission)
ai_fills.subtitle: Brief company description

ai_fills.items: 3-5 company values or strengths with icons and descriptions

after_columns.note: Only if business has gallery content (e.g. portfolio, before/after, projects)

about.selection_guidance: Company story and credibility page. Include for most businesses. Skip if the user explicitly says no about page, or if the business is purely transactional (e.g. simple tool/utility).

blog_listing.name: Blog Listing Page
blog_listing.description: Blog index with sidebar filters and post grid. Structure is fixed — AI does NOT generate blog content, only the page layout/chrome.

ai_fills.note: This page is almost entirely data-driven. AI generates only the structural HTML wrapper. Blog posts come from API.

blog_listing.selection_guidance: Blog index page. Include if the business benefits from content marketing, news, articles, industry updates, or if the user mentions blog/news. Skip for businesses that don't produce written content.

blog_post.name: Blog Post Detail Page
blog_post.description: Single article view. Fully data-driven — AI generates the page structure, not the post content. Always included if blog_listing is selected.

sections.note: Single component manages entire page layout. No section wrappers.

ai_fills.note: Structure-only generation. All content comes from get.blogPosts API. AI fills: social link choices, label translations, comment form labels.

alpine_page_state.data.post: Single post object with full content

page_endpoints.description: Fetches the single post. The post data includes embedded related_posts and tags arrays.

blog_post.selection_guidance: Individual blog article page. MUST be included if blog_listing is selected — they are paired. Never include without blog_listing.

contacts.name: Contacts Page
contacts.description: Contact page with Google Map embed, contact information cards, and contact form.

ai_fills.zoom: 14-16 for street level, 10-12 for city level

ai_fills.heading: Contact info heading
ai_fills.items: Contact cards from business_context.contact — phone (tel: link), email (mailto: link), address (plain text)

contacts.selection_guidance: Contact page with map, info cards, and form. Include if the business has a physical location, phone number, email, or wants customers to reach out. Skip for purely online businesses that don't want direct contact.

cookie_policy.name: Cookie Policy Page
cookie_policy.description: Legal page with cookie usage information. Always included. AI generates the full legal text using the static_content_block component.

ai_fills.title: Cookie policy title
ai_fills.content_blocks: Full cookie policy text. AI generates GDPR-compliant cookie policy using business_context placeholders.

cookie_policy.selection_guidance: Legal requirement for EU/Bulgarian businesses operating websites. Include for any business that serves EU users or uses cookies. Can be skipped only for non-EU businesses or if user explicitly opts out of legal pages.

gdpr_policy.name: GDPR Privacy Policy Page
gdpr_policy.description: Legal page with privacy policy. Always included. AI generates GDPR-compliant privacy policy.
required_sections: 2. Data categories with purpose and legal basis
gdpr_policy.selection_guidance: Privacy policy — legal requirement under GDPR for businesses handling personal data (forms, accounts, newsletters). Include for any business with user data collection. Can be skipped if no personal data is collected.

terms_conditions.name: Terms and Conditions Page
terms_conditions.description: Legal page with e-commerce terms. Always included. AI generates Bulgarian law-compliant T&C.

ai_fills.title: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS (centered, h1)
ai_fills.content_blocks: Full terms and conditions text with numbered articles (Art.) and Roman numeral sections.

content_guidelines.description: AI generates e-commerce terms and conditions compliant with Bulgarian law (Consumer Protection Act, E-Commerce Act, Electronic Communications Act). Content uses numbered articles (Art.) and Roman numeral sections.
formatting_notes: Sections numbered with Roman numerals (I, II, III...)
Articles numbered as Art. 1, Art. 2... with sub-paragraphs as (1), (2)...

Article references use

Art. N. (M) text

pattern
Some headings centered via text-center class or <h2> tags for appendices

terms_conditions.selection_guidance: Terms of service — legal requirement for e-commerce and service businesses in Bulgaria (Consumer Protection Act). Include for any business selling products/services online. Can be skipped for purely informational sites.

login.name: Login Page
login.description: User authentication page. Always included. Full-screen centered card with email/password form.
login.selection_guidance: User authentication page. Include if the business needs user accounts (e-commerce, bookings, memberships, dashboards). Skip for simple informational/brochure sites with no user accounts.

userpage.name: User Dashboard Page
userpage.description: User account management. Always included. 8-tab dashboard with profile, orders, addresses, etc. Mostly data-driven — AI generates structure and labels.

sections.note: Single component manages entire page layout. No section wrappers.

ai_fills.tab labels: Localized tab names (Profile, Invoice, Orders, etc.)
ai_fills.form labels: Localized field labels for all tab forms

userpage.selection_guidance: User dashboard with profile, orders, addresses. Include if login is selected AND the business has e-commerce, appointments, or account management. Skip if login is not included or accounts are minimal.

checkout.name: Checkout Page
checkout.description: Order placement page. Full two-column layout: left side has numbered step cards (customer data, delivery, payment, GDPR/terms), right side is a sticky dark cart panel with order summary, promo code, and totals. Standalone full-screen layout — no is-section wrappers. Four standalone apiData loader divs must appear before the main x-data wrapper.

sections.note: Single component manages the entire page. No section wrappers (section_wrapper_override: none). Four standalone top-level apiData divs must appear BEFORE the main x-data wrapper div.

ai_fills.submit_text: Localized order submit button text (e.g. 'Приключване на поръчката')
ai_fills.step_1_heading: Customer data step heading (e.g. 'Данни за клиента')
ai_fills.terms_label: Terms checkbox label with link to /bg/terms-conditions
ai_fills.gdpr_label: GDPR checkbox label with link to /bg/gdpr
ai_fills.econt_placeholder: Econt office picker placeholder (e.g. 'Изберете офис на Еконт')
ai_fills.speedy_placeholder: Speedy office picker placeholder (e.g. 'Изберете офис на Спиди')
ai_fills.comment_placeholder: Cart comment textarea placeholder (e.g. 'Добавете бележка към поръчката')

platform_data_used: data.cart — available globally, platform loads cart on all pages for logged-in users
data.user — available globally, platform loads user profile on all pages

component_interactions.action: selectedAddress = address; pickAddress = false; addAddress = false

component_interactions.trigger: Plugin auto-attaches to id='address' (input), id='location' (button), id='map' (div)
component_interactions.action: Populates data.location.* automatically. NO <script> tags needed — platform loads the plugin globally.

local_x_data.comment: '' — order comment, also bound to hidden input[name=comment]
local_x_data.selectedAddress: undefined — selected address object from data.my_addresses

alpine_page_state.data.settings.logo: Used as cart product image fallback

generation_notes: Do NOT output <script> or <link> tags for Google Maps or the geolocation plugin — the platform loads them globally. See component_catalog common_mistakes #13.

The four apiData loader divs MUST appear before the main x-data wrapper: Payments.get_payment_methods (combined with x-init redirect guard), Econt.get_offices, Speedy.get_speedy, User.get_my_addresses.
keyname attribute on promo actions is lowercase: keyname='cart' (not keyName).
Cart product image field is product.images[0].image_10x10 — NOT url_10x10.
data.cart and data.user do NOT need their own apiData loader — they are globally available.
checkout.selection_guidance: Order checkout page. Include if the business has an online shop (e-commerce). Should be paired with login and userpage. Skip for service businesses, brochure sites, or any business without an online storefront.

header.name: Site Header
header.description: Global navigation header. Generated AFTER page selection to reflect the correct nav links.

cta_button.href: /bg/contacts (only if contacts page was selected, otherwise first relevant page)

dynamic_nav_generation.description: The AI generates the static home link. Dynamic menu items come from get.menu API endpoint at runtime.
dynamic_nav_generation.nav_items_source: API — the platform populates menu items from the CMS. The AI does NOT hardcode navigation links except the static home link and CTA.

meta.note: Rendered on every page by site shell

footer.name: Site Footer
footer.description: Global footer. Generated AFTER page selection to include correct nav and legal links.

ai_fills.brand_column.title: business_context.company_name
ai_fills.brand_column.tagline: Short company tagline from business context
ai_fills.nav_columns: 1-3 link columns. Links MUST only reference pages that were selected in step 2.
ai_fills.bottom_bar.legal_links: Links to cookie, gdpr, terms pages — only those that were generated

link_validation.description: Every href in the footer MUST correspond to a page that was selected in step 2.
link_validation.rule: For each link, check: is the target route in selected_page_ids? If not, remove the link.

meta.note: Rendered on every page by site shell

output_manifest.description: After generation, the orchestrator produces this manifest listing all generated files.

validation_rules.description: Rules applied after generation to validate AI output against the component catalog.

checks.description: Every component used in generated HTML must exist in component_catalog.json
Every apiData/apidata attribute must reference an endpoint defined in the catalog
All Alpine.js directives must be in validation_rules.alpine_js.directives_allowed
All alpineListeners() calls must reference listeners in validation_rules.alpine_js.listeners_allowed
Every section must use a wrapper type defined in global.section_wrappers
No external scripts, stylesheets, or images except allowed exceptions
No <script> tags. Only inline Alpine handlers allowed.
All footer hrefs must point to pages in selected_pages
Header CTA href must point to a page in selected_pages
All tags must have alt attributes
All components marked required:true in the page type must be present in output
Cookie, GDPR, and Terms pages must contain all required_sections
